prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response: In the year of our Lord, I find myself summoned to record the mysterious harbingers of profit and loss as they pertain to a guild of software artificers, whose wares are conjured not from the earth, but from the very ether itself. Verily, they speak oft of “subscriptions,” a curious notion where one must pay a recurring tithe for continued access to their enchanted scrolls that do nothing but reside upon the boxes of glass and light. Their ledger, a parchment filled with numbers that dance around like sprites in a midsummer grove, claims that a 25% increase in “MRR” hath been achieved, though I suspect this to be some sorcery of rhetoric rather than a boon bestowed by honest toil. Lo, I scribble these confounding revelations whilst pondering why their knights of customer service wield but a digital quill to quell the grievances of the townsfolk, rather than a sword and shield in the noble tradition of our valiant defenders.
